feature_name: v1.17.0 "Polish" -- an 8-item bug-fix/polish bundle following v1.16.0 "Watch experience" (mobile horizontal-overflow root cause, watch-page scroll-jump, quicker delete flow, watch-page autoplay + ended-reset refinements, PWA force-close/resume, stuck one-off download modal, download icon glyph, subscription default download count)
current_tasks_note: IMPLEMENTATION STARTED (2026-07-06). EM finalized the principal-engineer's 7-task breakdown (T1=FR-1+FR-2, T2=FR-3, T3=FR-4, T4=FR-5, T5=FR-6, T6=FR-7, T7=FR-8) unchanged in structure. T1 is the anchor (joint mobile-layout investigation) and can start immediately; T2, T3, T5, T6, T7 are largely independent/parallelizable and start NOW alongside T1. T4 (FR-5 PWA lifecycle) is BLOCKED / pending-decision: the coordinator is asking Dean in parallel about the background-audio-vs-pause-on-lock tradeoff (design FR-5 flag) before any code is written -- T4 is NOT started and has NO active go-inbox. Review tier: coordinator overrode the PM's uniform LIGHTER recommendation to require the TWO-REVIEWER GATE on the four security-sensitive-touchpoint tasks T2 (delete-endpoint reuse), T4 (lifecycle events), T5 (modal teardown / innerHTML-XSS surface), T7 (yt-dlp config bounds); T1, T3, T6 remain single-QA LIGHTER. Dean's on-device pass remains the documented ARBITER for T1 (FR-1/FR-2) and T4 (FR-5). Inbox files written task-suffixed (software-developer-T1.md, -T2, -T3, -T5, -T6, -T7) since tasks run in parallel; -T4 written as a HELD placeholder (do-not-start). Coordinator owns ALL git; SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 test/lint and do NOT commit. PRIOR DISCOVERY NOTE: product-manager authored the exec plan with 8 FRs / 53 tagged acceptance criteria (grouped per-FR + a cross-cutting group), grounded directly against the current v1.16.0-shipped code (watch.js delete flow, player.js resume/autoplay/ended handling, common.js one-off modal, server.js autoplayNext + DELETE endpoint, lib/ytdlp/config.js DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS, style.css icon-set + mobile overflow history) and ROADMAP.md's 'Bugs & regressions' section. product-manager authored the exec plan with 8 FRs / 53 tagged acceptance criteria (grouped per-FR + a cross-cutting group), grounded directly against the current v1.16.0-shipped code (watch.js delete flow, player.js resume/autoplay/ended handling, common.js one-off modal, server.js autoplayNext + DELETE endpoint, lib/ytdlp/config.js DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS, style.css icon-set + mobile overflow history) and ROADMAP.md's 'Bugs & regressions' section. FR-1 (mobile horizontal-overflow root cause) is the PRIORITY anchor item; FR-2 (watch-page scroll-jump) is flagged as a SHARED-ROOT-CAUSE investigation with FR-1, not a separately-designed fix. Two open decisions surfaced for Dean (not blocking): FR-3(a) toast-vs-nothing after delete; FR-7's icon-fix approach (no download SVG asset exists in any icon set today, so this is not the one-line fix it may appear -- PM recommends adding a proper multi-set SVG asset for consistency with every other chrome icon). FR-8's 'which knob' question was resolved by inspection (DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS in lib/ytdlp/config.js), not left open. Recommends a LIGHTER single-QA no-regression review tier for all 8 items (no security guard changes, no new architecture) with Dean's on-device pass as the arbiter specifically for FR-1/FR-2/FR-5. Advancing to Design next. Reported back to EM; did not advance stage or commit/push (git is coordinator-owned).
branch: feature/v1.17-polish (off main, which is at v1.16.0; coordinator owns ALL git)
base: main (v1.16.0). NOTE: the working tree is currently on branch feat/icon-sets; the coordinator owns ALL git and will create feature/v1.17-polish off main before implementation. Prior shipped records live in docs/exec-plans/completed/ (most recently v1.16-watch-experience).
git_ownership_note: Coordinator owns ALL git. SDEs must NOT commit/stage/push. SDE reports files-changed + test output; coordinator commits per task.
node_toolchain_note: Node 22 is the toolchain standard (engines >=20). build-verify MUST run tests on Node 22; fnm/nvm PATH export needed before any npm/node command in this repo per CLAUDE.md.
process_plan: FULL PIPELINE, RIGHT-SIZED. This is a polish/bug-fix bundle -- no security-guard changes, no new architecture -- so PM recommends a LIGHTER single-QA no-regression review tier for all 8 items (no two-reviewer/adversarial gate needed, unlike v1.16.0's FR-1/FR-5). Security-sensitive TOUCHPOINTS (not guard changes) still get focused review attention: FR-3's interaction with the delete endpoint's existing 409/removeAnyway contract, FR-6's modal-teardown fix (must not introduce an innerHTML XSS surface while fixing the stuck-overlay bug), FR-8's yt-dlp config bounds/validation (must stay unchanged, only the default constant moves). Dean's ON-DEVICE pass is the documented, non-negotiable arbiter for FR-1 (mobile horizontal-overflow), FR-2 (scroll-jump), and FR-5 (iOS PWA force-close lifecycle) specifically -- these are CSS-layout/iOS-lifecycle bugs with no headless-browser test infra in this repo (per docs/RELIABILITY.md), so on-device verification is the only real arbiter, not a testing gap. EM/Design to confirm the review-tier recommendation at Task-breakdown time. Ship cadence: merge -> tag v1.17.0 -> Docker publish -> Dean's on-device pass (esp. FR-1/FR-2/FR-5).
hard_constraints: NO REGRESSIONS. Every item is additive/corrective on top of existing v1.16.0 behavior -- the persistent shell, docked mini-player, prev/next, existing autoplay-OFF default, existing pre-delete confirmation modal, existing resume-overlay logic for non-autoplay navigation, and the one-off download flow's successful-submit path must all keep working unchanged except where an FR explicitly changes them.

FR-1/FR-2 must be investigated TOGETHER as a shared-root-cause hypothesis before Design proposes two separate fixes; document whichever outcome (one fix closes both, or they genuinely diverge) in the Decision log.

FR-3: DELETE /api/videos/:id's existing behavior (success, 404, 500, and the read-only-mount EROFS/EACCES -> 409 + removeAnyway opt-in path) must not change -- only client UI/UX around it changes. No new client-side exposure of removeAnyway without first having seen the 409.

FR-3(a) is narrowly scoped to the POST-delete success alert('File deleted successfully.') in public/js/watch.js -- the existing pre-delete 'Confirm Permanent Deletion' showConfirmModal step is a legitimate guard and is NOT in scope for removal.

FR-4: autoplay stays OFF by default; the existing desktop-only initial-load autoplay posture (isMobileViewport() gate in player.js's handleResumePlayback) is unchanged. The FR-4(c) ended->currentTime=0 reset applies to EVERY completed video, independent of the autoplay setting -- it is not conditioned on autoplay being ON.
FR-6: the modal-teardown fix must not introduce an innerHTML XSS surface -- buildOneOffModal's existing DOM-node-based (non-innerHTML) construction must be preserved for any new/changed code.

FR-8: only the DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS fallback constant in lib/ytdlp/config.js changes (25 -> 3). The 0=unlimited semantics, existing bounds/validation (non-negative integer), and the FILETUBE_YTDLP_MAX_VIDEOS ENV-override behavior are all unchanged. No retroactive change to any already-persisted subscription's maxVideos.

follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler), CommonJS server, 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes; textContent over innerHTML for NEW dynamic strings; node:test for new/regression tests; lint 0 warnings; NO new runtime dependencies.

Preserve the era-theme system (CSS custom properties / --vars). Any new CSS (trash-can affordance, watch-page autoplay toggle, an FR-3(a) toast if chosen, any FR-1/FR-2 layout fix) must use existing theme tokens; no hardcoded colors that break the 2005/2009/2014/2021 eras or light/dark modes.

No headless-browser/E2E test infrastructure exists in this repo (per docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- this bounds what FR-1/FR-2/FR-5 can claim as [UNIT]/[INTEGRATION]; Dean's on-device pass is the documented arbiter for those three, not a rigor gap. Design should still extract and unit-test any pure helper the fix produces (an overflow-source check, a scroll-position helper, a resume-overlay-suppression flag, a lifecycle-event decision helper for FR-5, an arm/disarm state machine for FR-3(b)).

product_forks_for_dean.fr3a_toast_vs_nothing: OPEN, non-blocking. RECOMMEND a brief, non-blocking, auto-dismissing toast (era-themed) after a successful delete, rather than nothing at all -- gives some feedback without reintroducing the blocking-modal friction Dean objected to. 'Nothing' is equally valid per Dean's own framing. Needs Dean's pick before/during Design; proceed with the toast recommendation if no answer arrives.
product_forks_for_dean.fr7_icon_fix_approach: OPEN, non-blocking. RECOMMEND adding a proper download.svg Material Symbol across the outlined/rounded/filled icon sets (with the existing auto-per-era wiring), matching every other chrome icon's treatment -- the more consistent, durable fix. Alternative: swap only the fixed-Unicode glyph (currently \2B07) for a better-looking Unicode download glyph, matching the cheaper .icon-star/.icon-shuffle precedent (no new SVG asset, less work). IMPORTANT: neither option is the 'trivial icon-name fix' Dean's framing implies -- no download/file_download SVG asset exists in ANY icon set today, confirmed by reading the codebase. Flagging so Design scopes this correctly rather than under-estimating it. Needs Dean's pick or Design's judgment call.
product_forks_for_dean.fr8_which_knob: RESOLVED by inspection, not an open fork. DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS in lib/ytdlp/config.js (currently 25) is the single source of the effective default for BOTH a blank 'Max videos' field on a new subscription AND the FILETUBE_YTDLP_MAX_VIDEOS ENV fallback -- changing it to 3 satisfies Dean's stated intent ('a fresh subscription grabs the last 3 by default') with one edit. No further decision needed.

1.title: Mobile page-sizing / horizontal-overflow root cause (PRIORITY, anchor of the branch)
1.review_weight: PM-recommended LIGHTER (single-QA no-regression) -- but Dean on-device is the ARBITER given no headless-browser test infra
1.current_behavior_confirmed: Dean reports the page-wide horizontal-overflow signature (pinch-zoom-out 'fixes' the layout but shrinks the header search bar) -- classic evidence of an element wider than 100vw forcing mobile Safari to zoom the whole page out. style.css already carries several defensive min-width:0 fixes on the header/search box (lines ~308/315/324/622/1283) from prior rounds, plus a mobile .player-container max-height:45vh cap (line ~2227) and mobile sort-controls-row overflow fixes (line ~2128-2151) -- suggesting this class of bug has regressed more than once. Likely reintroduced by v1.16.0's header/search-box-on-more-shells changes or the new fixed #player-dock/persistent-shell CSS (style.css line ~658 #player-dock).
1.fix: Root-cause the exact offending element/rule (find what exceeds 100vw / breaks the viewport on mobile Safari), bisect to the regressing change where feasible, and fix it at the source -- not another overflow-x/min-width band-aid layered on top of the existing ones. Folds in the older ROADMAP items 'Mobile player oversized/not iOS-sized' and 'Mobile UI scale polish' (sort-controls row overflow, cards reading large) as likely-same-root-cause.
files: public/css/style.css (header/search-box CSS, #player-dock CSS, .player-container 45vh cap, mobile sort-controls-row CSS)
1.arbiter: Dean on-device (iOS Safari, phone width) -- no headless-browser infra exists to automate this

2.title: Watch-page scroll-jump (SHARED-ROOT-CAUSE hypothesis with item 1)
2.review_weight: PM-recommended LIGHTER -- Dean on-device is the ARBITER
2.current_behavior_confirmed: From ROADMAP: on certain videos, scrolling UP makes the bottom panel jump to the middle of the screen; scrolling down snaps back; a refresh fixes it. Intermittent/video-dependent -- likely a sticky/position-interaction CSS or layout/scroll-position bug on the watch page, same era/family as item 1.
2.fix: MUST be investigated TOGETHER with item 1 before proposing a fix -- do not treat as two independent patches without first ruling out a shared cause. Reproduce, document the trigger condition, then fix (jointly or separately, documented either way).
files: public/js/watch.js / public/js/player.js (any scroll-position-affecting logic, e.g. resume overlay, docked-player transitions)
2.arbiter: Dean on-device -- video-dependent, likely needs the specific previously-reported videos to repro

3.title: Quicker delete flow (success-alert removal + card trash-can arm/second-tap)
3.review_weight: LIGHTER (single-QA no-regression); FR-3's interaction with the delete endpoint's existing 409/removeAnyway contract gets focused review attention
3.current_behavior_confirmed: public/js/watch.js's deleteBtn handler (~line 605-631): 'Confirm Permanent Deletion' showConfirmModal guard (KEEP, unchanged) -> DELETE /api/videos/:id -> on success, alert('File deleted successfully.') (THIS is the friction to remove) -> navigate home. public/js/main.js (home/library cards) has NO delete affordance today -- item 3(b) is net-new. server.js's DELETE /api/videos/:id (~line 1945) already has the EROFS/EACCES -> 409 + removeAnyway opt-in path, but the CLIENT has no UI for it at all today (pre-existing gap, not required to close in this round).
3.fix: (a) Remove the post-delete success alert; a non-blocking auto-dismissing toast or nothing (Dean's pick, see fork). (b) Add a trash-can icon to home/library cards; first tap arms (inline are-you-sure state, no delete); second tap on the armed control deletes; tapping elsewhere disarms. No full modal for this new path.
files: public/js/main.js (NEW: card trash-can affordance + arm/disarm state)
public/css/style.css (trash-can + arm-state + optional toast styling, era tokens)
3.arbiter: code/test-verifiable (delete endpoint contract, arm/disarm logic) + Dean on-device for feel

4.title: Autoplay on the watch page + refinements (visible toggle, skip resume-overlay on autoplay-advance, reset currentTime=0 on ended)
4.current_behavior_confirmed: autoplayNext is a v1.16.0 db.settings boolean, OFF by default, toggled ONLY via a buried Settings-page checkbox (#autoplay-next-check in public/setup.html, wired in public/js/setup.js ~line 446/624). player.js's handleResumePlayback (~line 412) shows the resume overlay whenever savedProgress > 5, with NO distinction for whether the load was triggered by handleAutoplayNext (~line 534) vs. a normal navigation. wireHostListeners' 'ended' handler (~line 577) already does saveProgressToServer(0) + clearProgressInterval() on 'ended' (v1.16.0 C2 fix) -- but does NOT touch mediaPlayer.currentTime, so the LIVE player position is left wherever playback ended.
4.fix: (a) Add a visible autoplay toggle on the watch page near prev/next, backed by the SAME autoplayNext setting (must round-trip with the Settings-page toggle). (b) When handleAutoplayNext triggers a load, suppress the resume-overlay prompt for that load (auto-resume/play directly) -- normal navigations to a video with saved progress must still show it. (c) On 'ended', also reset mediaPlayer.currentTime = 0 (independent of the autoplay setting -- applies to every completed video), alongside the existing saveProgressToServer(0).
files: public/js/player.js (handleResumePlayback: suppress-overlay flag on autoplay-triggered loads; wireHostListeners' ended handler: mediaPlayer.currentTime = 0)
4.arbiter: code/test-verifiable (settings round-trip, ended-reset logic) + Dean on-device for the resume-overlay-suppression feel

5.title: PWA force-close doesn't stop playback + awkward resume
5.review_weight: LIGHTER -- but Dean's iOS-PWA on-device pass is the ARBITER; no headless-browser infra to automate iOS PWA lifecycle
5.current_behavior_confirmed: player.js wires only a 'visibilitychange' listener (~line 280) that RE-ASSERTS Media Session metadata/state on RETURN to foreground (document.visibilityState === 'visible') -- there is no pagehide/freeze handling and no pause-on-hidden logic today. iOS PWA background-audio + Media Session keeps the session alive past the app's visible closure; on reopen the resume drops back mid-stream rather than through the resume-overlay re-entry.
5.fix: Wire pause + persist-progress to pagehide / visibilitychange-going-hidden / freeze (whichever combination PE determines is most reliable for the iOS-PWA-force-close case) so audio does not outlive the app. On reopen, the resume overlay must be the re-entry point (not an auto-mid-play). Must not regress the existing visibilitychange foreground re-assert for ordinary backgrounding (not a force-close).
files: public/js/player.js (new pagehide/freeze/visibilitychange-hidden handler alongside the existing foreground-reassert one)
5.arbiter: Dean on-device, specifically an installed iOS PWA (home-screen) force-close-during-playback test

6.title: Stuck one-off download modal (backdrop-tap-without-URL leaves page dimmed/unresponsive)
6.review_weight: LIGHTER; the modal-teardown fix gets focused review attention for innerHTML/XSS regressions
6.current_behavior_confirmed: public/js/common.js's buildOneOffModal (~line 933) and decideOneOffTerminalAction (~line 1036) build the one-off download modal as real DOM nodes (comment at ~line 844 notes textContent-not-innerHTML is deliberate). Dean/ROADMAP report tapping the backdrop WITHOUT submitting a URL leaves the page dimmed and unresponsive to touch -- the teardown path on backdrop-dismiss-without-submit does not fully tear down (DOM removal / listener cleanup / a stuck overlay class).
6.fix: Ensure backdrop-tap (or an explicit close control) ALWAYS fully tears down the modal (removes backdrop+modal nodes, clears any listeners, restores touch/interaction) regardless of whether a URL was submitted -- one teardown code path, not divergent submit-vs-dismiss paths.
6.arbiter: unit-test-verifiable (DOM state after dismiss) + Dean on-device mobile confirmation

7.title: Download icon glyph fix
7.current_behavior_confirmed: .icon-download::before { content: "\2B07"; } (style.css ~line 1793) -- a fixed Unicode DOWNWARDS BLACK ARROW, explicitly documented as 'independent of the icon-set system,' unlike .icon-home/.icon-folder/.icon-delete/etc. which are real Material-Symbols SVG masks with per-icon-set (outlined/rounded/filled) + per-era variants (style.css ~line 1737-1880). NO download/file_download SVG asset exists in any of public/assets/icons/{,rounded/,filled/} today.
7.fix: Give it a proper, recognizable download glyph. Two options for PE/Dean to choose between (see product_forks_for_dean.fr7_icon_fix_approach): (a) add a real download.svg across all icon sets + era wiring (more consistent, more work), or (b) swap only the fixed-Unicode character for a better one (matches the .icon-star/.icon-shuffle precedent, less work). Neither is a true one-line fix given no asset exists yet -- do not under-scope this.
files: public/css/style.css (.icon-download rule, or new icon-set mask rules if the SVG route is chosen)
public/assets/icons/ (new download.svg x3 sets, if the SVG route is chosen)
test/unit (icon-set coverage extension, if the SVG route is chosen)
7.arbiter: code/visual review; Dean confirms which approach on-device across icon sets/eras if the SVG route is chosen

8.title: Subscription default download count 25 -> 3
8.review_weight: LIGHTER; yt-dlp config bounds/validation get focused review attention to confirm nothing loosened
8.current_behavior_confirmed: DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS = 25 in lib/ytdlp/config.js (~line 39), backing parseMaxVideos's fallback for FILETUBE_YTDLP_MAX_VIDEOS AND the effective default whenever a new subscription's 'Max videos' field (sub-add-maxvideos in lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html, placeholder 'Max videos (blank=default, 0=unlimited)') is left blank. 0=unlimited semantics and non-negative-integer validation already exist and are correct.
8.fix: Change DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS from 25 to 3. No other change -- 0=unlimited semantics, bounds/validation, and the ENV-override contract stay exactly as they are. This is a NEW-subscription default only; do not touch any already-persisted subscription's maxVideos.
files: test/unit (existing parseMaxVideos fallback-to-default tests, updated to assert 3)

pe_design_items: Route to Design after Discovery. Items needing PE design: 1 (mobile horizontal-overflow root-cause investigation), 2 (watch-page scroll-jump -- MUST be investigated jointly with item 1 first), 3 (delete-flow UX: toast-vs-nothing choice, card trash-can arm/disarm mechanism), 5 (PWA lifecycle: which event combination -- pagehide/visibilitychange-hidden/freeze -- most reliably covers the iOS force-close case), 7 (icon-fix approach: new SVG asset across all sets vs. improved Unicode glyph). Items 4, 6, 8 are more direct (settings/handler wiring, a modal-teardown bugfix, a single constant change) but still ride through Design for one cohesive plan.
cross_cutting: Items 1 and 2 are a joint investigation, not independent fixes -- Design must confirm or rule out a shared root cause before implementing either. Item 4's autoplay toggle and item 3's card-affordance both touch the watch page / home page UI surface but are otherwise independent. Item 8 is fully isolated to lib/ytdlp/config.js -- shares nothing with the client-side items. No new deps; era-theme CSS vars for all new controls; textContent-not-innerHTML for new dynamic strings; node:test; lint 0. No headless-browser/E2E infra exists (docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- items 1, 2, and 5 lean on Dean's on-device pass as the documented arbiter, not a testing gap; every other item ships fully code/test-verifiable ACs per docs/CONTRIBUTING.md's 'every new feature or bugfix ships with tests' standard.

tasks.title: FR-1 + FR-2 mobile layout (joint investigation)
tasks.description: Run the FR-1 console overflow diagnostic on home/watch/setup at 375-414px width and NAME the offending >100vw element BEFORE touching any CSS (mandatory; no overflow-x/min-width band-aid). Apply the source fix to the named offender (ranked suspect: the home #sort-select lacking max-width/min-width:0 inside .section-actions). Apply the FR-2 stable-top-offset fix (safe-area-inset baked into both header min-height AND .app-container padding-top shifts on iOS address-bar collapse). Re-verify the folded-in 'certain videos wrong-size' / 'cards read large' as symptoms of the same zoom-out. Document the joint-investigation outcome in the Decision log.
tasks.review_tier: LIGHTER single-QA; Dean on-device (iOS Safari) is the ARBITER
tasks.ordering: anchor; starts first; runs parallel to T2/T3/T5/T6/T7

tasks.title: FR-3 quicker delete flow
tasks.description: Add showToast(msg) helper to common.js (appends a .toast node, textContent only, ~2.5s auto-dismiss fade, era tokens) + .toast CSS. Remove the post-delete alert('File deleted successfully.') from watch.js DELETE-success branch, replace with showToast('File deleted.'); KEEP the pre-delete showConfirmModal('Confirm Permanent Deletion') guard unchanged. Add a card trash-can affordance to home/library cards in main.js renderMediaGrid via a single delegated AbortSignal-bound click listener on #video-grid; drive arm/disarm with a pure unit-tested reducer nextArmState(current, action) (idle+tap->armed no-delete; armed+tap->idle deleted; *+disarm->idle no-delete) + ~3s auto-disarm + disarm-on-outside-click. Reuse DELETE /api/videos/:id EXACTLY (no endpoint/contract change); never send removeAnyway without first seeing a 409.
tasks.review_tier: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (delete-endpoint reuse + card-delete security touchpoint)

tasks.title: FR-4 watch-page autoplay toggle + refinements
tasks.description: (a) Add a visible autoplay toggle to watch.html near .watch-prevnext, wired in watch.js to read from GET /api/settings.autoplayNext and write via POST /api/settings {autoplayNext} (server does a partial KNOWN_KEYS merge; same persisted field as the Settings-page toggle; sync via re-fetch on load; NO server change). (b) Suppress the resume overlay ONLY on an autoplay-advanced load: module-level one-shot flag in player.js set true in handleAutoplayNext before navigate(), consumed via a pure unit-tested helper shouldShowResumeOverlay({savedProgress, autoplayAdvance}) = savedProgress>5 && !autoplayAdvance; a normal navigation still shows the overlay unchanged. (c) On the 'ended' listener in wireHostListeners, after saveProgressToServer(0) add: if (!liveMode && mediaPlayer) mediaPlayer.currentTime = 0 -- applies to EVERY completed video regardless of the autoplay setting.
tasks.review_tier: LIGHTER single-QA; Dean on-device for resume-overlay-suppression feel

tasks.title: FR-5 PWA force-close: pause + persist, clean resume
tasks.description: BLOCKED / PENDING-DECISION -- DO NOT START. Blocked on a Dean product decision (design FR-5 flag): pausing on visibilitychange:hidden also stops screen-off/lock-screen background audio; there is no clean iOS PWA event isolating force-close from backgrounding. Coordinator is asking Dean in parallel whether to ship the all-three-event version (pagehide/freeze/visibilitychange:hidden -- recommended) or fall back to pagehide+freeze only for background-audio-continues behavior. When unblocked: add lifecycle listeners in player.js alongside the existing foreground visibilitychange re-assert (which stays intact); on the chosen events, if media is loaded+playing, mediaPlayer.pause() + saveProgressToServer(currentAbsTime()); extract pure unit-tested shouldPauseForLifecycleEvent(type, {hasMedia}); on reopen (fresh load) the standard handleResumePlayback overlay is the clean re-entry. Must not regress the existing foreground Media-Session re-assert for ordinary backgrounding.
tasks.review_tier: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (lifecycle events); Dean on-device iOS-PWA force-close is the ARBITER
tasks.ordering: HELD -- blocked pending Dean's FR-5 background-audio-vs-pause-on-lock decision; NOT started; no active go-inbox

tasks.title: FR-6 stuck one-off download modal teardown
tasks.description: Root cause confirmed: .oneoff-modal-backdrop sets display:flex with NO [hidden] override, so backdrop.hidden=true in closeModal does not actually hide the full-viewport fixed z-index:2100 overlay -- it stays painted and eats every touch. Fix (one teardown path): (1) CSS source fix -- add .oneoff-modal-backdrop[hidden]{display:none} (or fold display:flex into :not([hidden]), matching the .playlists-sheet-backdrop precedent; no !important). (2) Harden closeModal into a full teardown shared by all dismiss paths (backdrop tap, [x], Esc, done auto-close): backdrop.remove(), stopPolling(), null currentJobId AND modalState so openModal rebuilds fresh (the once-bound keydown Esc handler already guards on modalState&& so nulling makes it an inert no-op). Preserve the existing createElement/textContent construction -- introduce NO innerHTML.
tasks.review_tier: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (modal teardown; must not introduce an innerHTML/XSS surface)

tasks.title: FR-7 proper download icon across the icon sets
tasks.description: Add a real download Material-Symbols mask matching every other chrome icon. Add download.svg (Material Symbols download/file_download) to public/assets/icons/, .../rounded/, and .../filled/ sourced/licensed per public/assets/icons/README.md. Add .icon-download to: the base mask group, the outlined mask-image assignment, the @supports currentColor fill guard, the [data-icons=rounded] block, the [data-icons=filled] block, and the [data-icons=emoji] neutralize group + an emoji ::before (recommend U+1F4E5). REMOVE the old fixed-glyph rule .icon-download::before{content:'\2B07'}. Existing usages (injectOneOffDownloadButtonIfEnabled) already emit <i class=icon-download> and need no change. Extend the icon-set unit test (style of test/unit/shuffle-rescan-icon.test.js / icon-assets.test.js) to assert the new asset+mask coverage across sets.
tasks.review_tier: LIGHTER single-QA; visual review across icon sets/eras/light-dark

tasks.title: FR-8 subscription default DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS 25 -> 3
tasks.description: In lib/ytdlp/config.js change DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS = 25 to 3 and update the adjacent '25 was chosen' comment. NOTHING ELSE MOVES: parseMaxVideos, the 0=unlimited semantics, the non-negative-integer validation, and the FILETUBE_YTDLP_MAX_VIDEOS ENV-fallback contract are all unchanged; the constant is the single knob backing both the blank-field UI default and the ENV fallback. NO migration/backfill -- existing persisted subscriptions keep their stored maxVideos. Update the parseMaxVideos fallback-to-default unit tests to assert 3. Optionally refresh the sub-add-maxvideos placeholder hint in lib/ytdlp/views/subscriptions.html (cosmetic, not blocking).

acceptance_verdict: ACCEPT-WITH-NOTES -- 38/53 ACs fully MET, 15/53 PARTIAL (all MANUAL/on-device-arbiter items for FR-1, FR-2, FR-4(b/c) feel, FR-5, FR-7 visual polish -- exactly the class of check the plan itself designated as resolvable only by Dean's on-device pass), 0/53 FAIL. See the exec plan's new Acceptance section for the full per-FR/per-AC breakdown.

history.note: PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Acceptance for v1.17.0 'Polish.' Implementation was reported complete (build green 1163/1163 on Node 22, lint clean; QA APPROVED; adversarial review found 2 bugs -- an autoplayAdvancePending resume-skip flag leak in FR-4b and a force-close progress-persist gap in FR-5's pagehide save -- both fixed and re-confirmed FIXED-CLEAN). Verified each of the 53 tagged ACs directly against the delivered code across all 8 FRs: read style.css (FR-1 .sort-select/.section-actions min-width fix; FR-2 --mobile-header-h/--mobile-bottom-nav-h consolidation, both scoped inside the 768px media query so desktop is unaffected), watch.js + common.js + main.js (FR-3 showToast + nextArmState reducer + card trash-can with 5 confirmed disarm paths: timeout/outside-click/scroll/re-render/destroy; DELETE /api/videos/:id and its 409/removeAnyway contract confirmed textually unchanged), watch.html + watch.js + player.js (FR-4 autoplay toggle round-tripping the same autoplayNext KNOWN_KEYS setting, shouldShowResumeOverlay + captureAutoplayAdvanceForLoad fixing the leak, currentTime=0 on 'ended' guarded off liveMode), player.js (FR-5 shouldPauseForLifecycleEvent wired to pagehide/freeze/visibilitychange:hidden, video-pauses/audio-continues split confirmed matching Dean's locked 'smart' behavior, keepalive:true fixing the force-close persist gap, foreground visibilitychange re-assert confirmed untouched), common.js (FR-6 closeModal full teardown -- backdrop.remove()+stopPolling()+null modalState/currentJobId -- plus the [hidden] CSS override, no innerHTML introduced), style.css + public/assets/icons/{,rounded/,filled/}/download.svg (FR-7 real Material-Symbols mask wired into every icon-set rule group, old \2B07 removed), lib/ytdlp/config.js + subscriptions.html (FR-8 DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS 25->3, 0=unlimited/bounds/ENV-override confirmed unchanged, placeholder hint refreshed). Cross-checked all corresponding test files (card-delete-arm-state, player-resume-overlay, player-lifecycle, oneoff-modal-teardown, download-icon, ytdlp-config's updated assertion) exist and directly cover the extracted pure helpers, including an explicit end-to-end regression test for the fixed autoplayAdvancePending leak. Result: 38/53 ACs fully MET (all UNIT/INTEGRATION/PROCESS criteria, zero FAILs); 15/53 PARTIAL -- exclusively the MANUAL/on-device-arbiter checks for FR-1 (on-device iOS Safari overflow confirmation), FR-2 (on-device scroll-jump confirmation), FR-4 (resume-overlay-suppression on-screen feel), FR-5 (installed iOS PWA force-close pass), and FR-7 (cross-icon-set/era visual polish) -- precisely the class of check the exec plan itself designated as resolvable only by Dean's on-device pass, not a code/process gap. Confirmed Dean's stated constraints all honored in code: watch page/desktop unencumbered (FR-1/FR-2 fixes scoped inside the 768px media query only), card-delete guards against accidental taps (arm/confirm two-tap + 5 disarm paths), audio backgrounds while video pauses on PWA lifecycle events (shouldPauseForLifecycleEvent's explicit isAudio branch), subscription default is now 3 (DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS + updated test assertion). Wrote a new Acceptance section to docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-06-v1.17-polish.md with the full per-FR/per-AC verdict table and an ACCEPT-WITH-NOTES recommendation. Set stage to acceptance-complete and completed_tasks to all 7 tasks (T1-T7; T4 was the previously-HELD FR-5 task, now confirmed implemented). Did not edit any application/test code and did not commit/push (git remains coordinator-owned). Recommending the EM advance to Done, contingent on Dean's on-device pass as the documented, already-anticipated final arbiter for FR-1/FR-2/FR-5/FR-7's visual polish -- not a new blocking condition.

ENGINEERING-MANAGER finalized the principal-engineer's Task breakdown and advanced stage discovery->design->tasks->implementation (Design + proposed Task breakdown were appended to docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-06-v1.17-polish.md by PE). Accepted the PE's 7-task decomposition UNCHANGED in structure: T1=FR-1+FR-2 (joint mobile-layout, anchor, mandatory console diagnostic to NAME the >100vw offender before any CSS change, no overflow-x band-aid), T2=FR-3 (showToast helper + remove watch-page success alert + card trash-can arm/disarm via pure nextArmState reducer, reuse DELETE /api/videos/:id unchanged), T3=FR-4 (watch-page autoplay toggle round-tripping autoplayNext + shouldShowResumeOverlay suppress-on-autoplay-advance + currentTime=0 on 'ended'), T4=FR-5 (PWA pause+persist on pagehide/freeze/visibilitychange:hidden + shouldPauseForLifecycleEvent helper), T5=FR-6 (.oneoff-modal-backdrop[hidden] override + full closeModal teardown, no innerHTML), T6=FR-7 (real download.svg across outlined/rounded/filled/emoji + remove the \2B07 Unicode arrow), T7=FR-8 (DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS 25->3, new-subs-only, bounds/0=unlimited/ENV contract unchanged). ORDERING: T1 anchor + T2/T3/T5/T6/T7 start in parallel NOW; T4 is HELD/blocked pending a Dean product decision on FR-5 background-audio-vs-pause-on-lock (coordinator asking in parallel) -- NOT started, no active go-inbox. REVIEW TIER: EM overrode the PM's uniform LIGHTER recommendation to require a TWO-REVIEWER GATE on the four security-sensitive-touchpoint tasks (T2 delete-endpoint reuse, T4 lifecycle events, T5 modal-teardown/innerHTML-XSS, T7 yt-dlp config bounds); T1/T3/T6 remain single-QA LIGHTER. Dean on-device is the documented ARBITER for T1 (FR-1/FR-2) and T4 (FR-5). Wrote task-suffixed self-contained inbox files: .state/inbox/software-developer-{T1,T2,T3,T5,T6,T7}.md (go) + software-developer-T4.md (HELD placeholder, do-not-start). Each notes: coordinator owns ALL git (SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 test/lint, do NOT commit/stage/push); Node 22 toolchain PATH is /tmp/claude-1000/-home-coder-projects-filetube/139c0e56-b545-4e8e-ba05-f892f6dd6d0d/scratchpad/node-v22.23.1-linux-x64/bin. Did not write application code or touch git.

PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Discovery for v1.17.0 'Polish,' an 8-item bug-fix/polish bundle initiated by Dean following v1.16.0 'Watch experience.' Branch feature/v1.17-polish off main (v1.16.0). Read docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-06-v1.16-watch-experience.md, ROADMAP.md's 'Bugs & regressions' section, docs/CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/RELIABILITY.md, and grounded every FR directly against the current code: public/js/watch.js's delete flow (Confirm-Permanent-Deletion modal KEPT, post-delete success alert REMOVED per FR-3a); public/js/player.js's handleResumePlayback/wireHostListeners/handleAutoplayNext/visibilitychange handling (FR-4b/c, FR-5); public/js/common.js's buildOneOffModal/decideOneOffTerminalAction (FR-6); server.js's autoplayNext setting plumbing and DELETE /api/videos/:id's existing EROFS/EACCES->409/removeAnyway contract (FR-3, FR-4a); lib/ytdlp/config.js's DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS=25 (FR-8); style.css's .icon-download fixed-Unicode glyph + the icon-set-axis mask system + the mobile min-width/45vh-cap overflow-fix history (FR-1, FR-7). Confirmed no conflict with any active exec plan (docs/exec-plans/active/ was empty before this file) or docs/exec-plans/tech-debt-tracker.md. Authored docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-06-v1.17-polish.md: Goal/Scope(FR-1..FR-8)/Out-of-scope/Constraints/Product-forks/grouped-tagged-Acceptance-criteria (FR-1 x7, FR-2 x4, FR-3 x7, FR-4 x9, FR-5 x5, FR-6 x4, FR-7 x4, FR-8 x6, Cross-cutting x7 = 53 criteria total). Flagged FR-1<->FR-2 as an explicit SHARED-ROOT-CAUSE joint-investigation requirement, not two independent fixes. Surfaced 2 open decisions for Dean (non-blocking, each with a recommendation): FR-3(a) toast-vs-nothing after delete; FR-7's icon-fix approach (no download SVG asset exists in any icon set today, so 'trivial icon-name fix' under-scopes it -- recommend a proper multi-set SVG asset). FR-8's 'which knob' question was resolved by inspection (DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS in lib/ytdlp/config.js), not left open. Recommended a LIGHTER single-QA no-regression review tier for all 8 items (no security-guard changes, no new architecture) with Dean's on-device pass as the documented arbiter specifically for FR-1/FR-2/FR-5 (no headless-browser/E2E infra exists per docs/RELIABILITY.md). Set artifacts.exec_plan/requirements/design all to the new exec plan path. Did NOT advance stage or touch git (coordinator-owned). Reporting back to EM for Design routing (/prep-pe-design).
